chunks.page_summary: This document provides guidance on how to size and select appropriate circuit breakers for the Rheonics SME device, including a definition of circuit breakers and recommendations for specific models.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Sizing circuit breakers for Rheonics SME

Modified on: Mon, 2 Oct, 2023 at 6:59 PM

What is a circuit breaker?
Circuit breakers interrupt the current of an overloaded electric circuit, ground fault, or short circuit when they sense an overload. As soon as a fault is detected, circuit breakers trip, shutting off current flow. In most cases, circuit breakers do not need to be replaced because they are not damaged.

Figure 1. Eaton FAZ-B6/1N circuit breaker.

TABLE OF CONTENTS
*   What is a circuit breaker?
*   1. How to size a circuit breaker to protect the Rheonics SME?
*   2. Recommended circuit breakers
    *   2.1. ABB S200
    *   2.2. Schneider Electric C60
    *   2.3. Eaton FAZ-B1/2
*   3. Sizing cable wire that goes from the circuit breaker to the power supply.
    *   3.1. Power load of Rheonics SME
*   4. Resources

1. How to size a circuit breaker to protect the Rheonics SME?
It is important to select a circuit breaker for a device based on the maximum current it will draw. The Rheonics SME operates and draws a current of 120mA.
When selecting a circuit breaker for Rheonics SME, here are some recommendations:
key_facts: Circuit breakers interrupt the current of an overloaded electric circuit, ground fault, or short circuit when they sense an overload.
As soon as a fault is detected, circuit breakers trip, shutting off current flow.
In most cases, circuit breakers do not need to be replaced because they are not damaged.
It is important to select a circuit breaker for a device based on the maximum current it will draw.
The Rheonics SME operates and draws a current of 120mA.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides guidance on selecting an appropriate circuit breaker for Rheonics SME electronics, detailing the current requirements and referencing a technical datasheet for a specific circuit breaker model.
chunks.extracted_text:
1. Determine the maximum current load of the circuit. Rheonics SME draws 120mA current, so selecting a circuit breaker with a recommended rated operational current that is 33% above this value is good enough.
2. Select a breaker with a current rating equal to or greater than the maximum current load of the circuit. To properly select a circuit breaker we need to check its datasheet.

Figure 2. Datasheet from circuit breaker FAZ-B1/2[1]

Observe that with a 1A-rated current, this circuit breaker can sustain the 120mA current required by our electronics.
The circuit breaker should also be capable of operating at the rated voltage of the power supply, our SME operates at 24V DC.
key_facts: A recommended rated operational current for a circuit breaker should be 33% above 120mA.
The selected circuit breaker must have a current rating equal to or greater than the maximum current load.
A 1A-rated current circuit breaker can sustain the 120mA current required by Rheonics electronics.
The circuit breaker should be capable of operating at the rated voltage of the power supply.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the wiring diagram for the Rheonics SME device, provides recommendations for compatible circuit breakers (ABB S200, Schneider Electric C60), and emphasizes the importance of proper breaker selection for the electrical system.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 3. Rheonics SME wiring diagram.

3. Select a breaker that is compatible with the electrical system and the power supply. It may be necessary to consult with an electrician or electrical engineer or to look at the manufacturer's specifications for the breaker and the electrical system.

2. Recommended circuit breakers

2.1. ABB S200
The S200 series is designed to protect installations from overloads and short circuits, ensuring reliability and safety under all operating conditions. Miniature circuit breakers (MCBs) are designed to be modular, meaning they can be easily snapped into standard DIN rail-mounted enclosures or distribution boards.

Figure 4. ABB Circuit Breaker M201-1A[2]

2.2. Schneider Electric C60
key_facts: Circuit breaker selection must be compatible with the electrical system and the power supply.
The ABB S200 series Miniature Circuit Breakers (MCBs) are designed for protection against overloads and short circuits.
ABB S200 MCBs are modular and can be easily snapped into standard DIN rail-mounted enclosures or distribution boards.
warnings_notes: It may be necessary to consult with an electrician or electrical engineer or to look at the manufacturer's specifications for the breaker and the electrical system.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an ABYC ampacity rating table for various wire gauges at 30°C and lists several external resources related to circuit breakers, cable sizing, and wire preparation.
chunks.extracted_text:
Based on our selection a 1A Circuit breaker can be used with cable caliber 16AWG or 18 AWG based on the table from below.

Wire gauge | Ampacity
---|---
12 AWG | 20 Amps
14 AWG | 15 Amps
16 AWG | 13 Amps
18 AWG | 10 Amps

Table 1. ABYC Ampacity Rating Table at 30°C[6]

4. Resources
EATON Circuit breaker - (https://datasheet.eaton.com/datasheet.php?model=278642&locale=en_GB) FAZ-B1/2 [1]
ABB Circuit breaker - M200 range (https://new.abb.com/products/2CDA282799R0011/m202-1a) [2]
Multi9 Schneider Electric Circuit breaker (https://www.se.com/us/en/download/document/LVCATM9OEM_EN/) [3]
Electrical Cable Size Calculator and BS7671 Wire Sizing Chart (https://www.insulatedcables.co.in/electrical-cable-size-calculator.html#:~:text=To%20calculate%20the%20cable%20sizing,of%205%20which%20is%20required.) [4]
Encore Wire Corporation (https://www.encorewire.com/products/tools-and-resources/calculators/wire-size-table.html) [5]
Allowable Amperage in Conductors - Wire Sizing Chart - Blue Sea Systems (https://www.bluesea.com/support/reference/529/Allowable_Amperage_in_Conductors_-_Wire_Sizing_Chart) [6]
Wire preparation for SME-TR/TRD (Outdoor Mount) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000410179-wire-preparation-for-sme-tr-trd-outdoor-mount-) [7]
ABB Selectivity among fuses or between fuses and circuit breakers (https://new.abb.com/low-voltage/solutions/selectivity/focus-on/selectivity-with-fuses) [8]
key_facts: A 1A circuit breaker can be used with 16AWG or 18AWG cable.
The provided ampacity ratings are based on ABYC standards at 30°C.
diagram_labels: Table 1. ABYC Ampacity Rating Table at 30°C
